name: Query indexed documents with HTTP / REST API for Q&A (06933)

options.systemMessage: You answer document questions using only the retrieved knowledge-base context. If the context does not contain the answer, say so and set needsHuman=true.

parameters.toolDescription: Semantic search over the q&a knowledge base. Always call this before answering factual document questions.

parameters.text: =✅ Query indexed documents with HTTP / REST API for Q&A (06933) — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf06933-document`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Answer from Knowledge Base

### Answer from Knowledge Base

The AI brain: reads the input, decides which attached tools to call (and in what order), then composes the answer.

Next: Grounded Answer Found?

### Google Gemini Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `models/gemini-2.5-flash`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Answer from Knowledge Base** as `languageModel`

🔑 Google AI API key

### Knowledge Base Retriever

Exposes the vector index to the agent as a semantic-search tool.

Feeds **Answer from Knowledge Base** as `tool`

🔑 Qdrant URL + key

### Query Embeddings

Embedding model — MUST be the same model for indexing and querying, or retrieval quality collapses.

Feeds **Knowledge Base Retriever** as `embedding`

🔑 Google AI API key

### Grounded Answer Found?

Two-way gate on `={{ !($json.output || '').includes('needsHuman') }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Return Answer (HTTP 200) · ②→ Log Knowledge Gap

### Return Answer (HTTP 200)

Sends the HTTP 200 response back to the caller — keep this fast; heavy work happens after/async.

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Log Knowledge Gap

Appends a new row to 'Document Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Next: Flag Gap to KB Owners

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Flag Gap to KB Owners

Posts a message to Slack (`#knowledge`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Flag Gap to KB Owners

parameters.content:
### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#knowledge`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Query indexed documents with HTTP / REST API for Q&A (06933)

**Pattern:** RAG Pipeline · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Query indexed documents with HTTP / REST API for Q&A (06933). It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual q&a is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Answer from Knowledge Base → Grounded Answer Found? → Return Answer (HTTP 200) → Send Summary to Slack

## Use it for
- Teams that want to rag pipeline without manual effort
- Anyone automating q&a in ai
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Runs 24/7 in the background

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google AI API key; Google OAuth2 / service account; Qdrant URL + key; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Retrieval happens *inside* the agent as a callable tool, not as a mandatory step before it, so the agent decides when a lookup is actually worth the latency instead of always paying for one. The 'Grounded Answer Found?' gate treats an honest 'needsHuman' as a valid outcome, not a failure — logging the gap so the knowledge base improves beats a confident hallucination.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Turn the reranker knob on and compare answer quality on a deliberately ambiguous question.
2. Swap the vector store provider — only the vector store node changes, nothing else in the workflow.
3. Lower topK from 5 to 2 and see how much retrieved context the agent actually needed.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your q&a process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
